alignments.rationale: The claim about emerging automated-design techniques to improve accessibility is preserved with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review covers multiple levels of interpretability including scientific, mathematical, statistical, and partial semantic interpretation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim set does not include the detailed enumeration of interpretability levels present in the preprint, indicating this specific result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The applications reviewed include food processing, agriculture, and health. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review includes applications of interpretable ML in food processing, agriculture, and health. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same list of application domains reviewed, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A visual-tactile fusion framework for transparent object grasping is proposed, which includes grasping position detection, tactile calibration, and visual-tactile fusion based classification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tactile calibration method and visual-tactile fusion classification method improve the grasping success rate by 36.7% compared to direct grasping. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the performance benefit of specific components (tactile calibration and fusion classification) rather than restating the full component-level contribution list, thus clarifying and focusing the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel grasping network named TGCNN shows good results in both synthetic and real scenes for grasping position detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed framework for grasping transparent objects. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about TGCNN performance in synthetic and real scenes is generalized in the publication into an overall validation of the framework, which broadens the focus and omits the network-specific detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tactile calibration method improves the success rate of grasping by 36.7% compared to direct grasping. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tactile calibration method and visual-tactile fusion classification method improve the grasping success rate by 36.7% compared to direct grasping. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the exact numeric improvement and attributes it to the same methods, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The visual-tactile fusion method improves the classification accuracy of transparent objects by 34%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification accuracy is improved by 39.1% with the proposed methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a larger numeric improvement (39.1% vs 34%), representing a revision of the quantified effect and a stronger claimed benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework greatly improves the grasping efficiency of transparent objects by synergizing the advantages of vision and touch. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed visual-tactile fusion framework significantly improves the grasping efficiency of transparent objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the improvement more assertively (higher certainty/result language) and uses stronger wording (significantly), amplifying confidence while preserving the original claim about improved efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shadow removal improves the visual quality and legibility of digital copies of documents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shadow removal improves the visual quality and legibility of digital copies of documents.
alignments.rationale: The two statements are identical in content and emphasis about shadow removal improving document visual quality and legibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Transformer-based model utilizes shadow context encoding and decoding in both shadow and shadow-free regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Transformer-based model for document shadow removal utilizes shadow context encoding and decoding in both shadow and shadow-free regions.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same technical contribution with only minor wording differences (explicitly mentioning document shadow removal).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model includes shadow detection and pixel-level enhancement in the whole coarse-to-fine process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model includes shadow detection and pixel-level enhancement in the whole coarse-to-fine process.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim verbatim about shadow detection and pixel-level enhancement in a coarse-to-fine pipeline.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model is competitive with state-of-the-art methods based on comprehensive benchmark evaluations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model is competitive with state-of-the-art methods based on comprehensive benchmark evaluations.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is restated identically in the publication about competitiveness against state-of-the-art methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing statistical methods can estimate a policy that instructs decision makers on treatment based on covariates like blood pressure and heart rate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing statistical methods can estimate a policy that instructs decision makers on treatment based on covariates like blood pressure and heart rate.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim about existing methods for policy estimation using covariates without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for the identification of parameters that change when moving from the standard of care to a new policy, enhancing interpretability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows for the identification of parameters that change when moving from the standard of care to a new policy, enhancing interpretability.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about identifying changed parameters for interpretability is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concept of 'relative sparsity' enables control over the number of parameters in the suggested policy that differ from the standard of care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concept of 'relative sparsity' enables control over the number of parameters in the suggested policy that differ from the standard of care.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same technical claim about relative sparsity controlling differing parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed criterion for selecting the tuning parameter $\text{λ}$ aids in deriving a policy that is easy to explain in the context of the current standard of care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed criterion for selecting the tuning parameter λ aids in deriving a policy that is easy to explain in the context of the current standard of care.
alignments.rationale: The criterion-for-λ claim is restated essentially unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work promotes the adoption of data-driven decision aids, which have the potential to improve health outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work promotes the adoption of data-driven decision aids, which have the potential to improve health outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates the certainty of the potential health-outcome benefit from medium to high, indicating a stronger expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance-driven design exploration framework enhances the capabilities of human designers through the use of a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CVAE overcomes the limitations of traditional iterative techniques by learning a differentiable mapping for a highly nonlinear design space.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the technical advantage of the CVAE (overcoming iterative techniques) rather than directly claiming enhancement of human designers, shifting the emphasis of the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CVAE serves as both a forward performance predictor for given design features and an inverse design feature predictor based on performance requests.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) serves as a forward performance predictor and an inverse design generator conditioned on a set of performance requests.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the dual forward/inverse role of the CVAE with equivalent meaning and conditioning language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sensitivity analysis is utilized to provide explainability and inform designers about the relationships between features and performances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework allows for informing designers about the relations of the model between features and performances.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that the framework provides explainability about feature-performance relationships; the publication wording is slightly more general but equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A case study demonstrated the framework's potential to act as a co-pilot for conceptual design studies of pedestrian bridges.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework proves its potential to serve as a future co-pilot for conceptual design studies of diverse civil structures and beyond.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claimed applicability from a pedestrian-bridge case study to a broader set of civil structures, clarifying and widening the intended scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed Bayesian semi-parametric model estimates the impact of dynamic treatment rules on survival among pediatric acute myeloid leukemia patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed Bayesian semiparametric model effectively estimates the potential survival probability under hypothetical dynamic ACT treatment strategies for pediatric AML patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim to focus specifically on estimating potential survival probability under hypothetical strategies, a clarification of the original impact-estimation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model accounts for confounding effects over time due to the non-randomized nature of ACT treatment in the clinical trial.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model accounts for the confounding effects of non-randomized ACT treatment on survival over time.
alignments.rationale: Both statements indicate the model adjusts for time-varying confounding from non-randomized ACT treatment; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A g-computation procedure is utilized to compute a posterior over potential survival probability adjusted for time-varying confounding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: G-computation is utilized to compute a posterior over potential survival probability that adjusts for time-varying confounding.
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim about using g-computation to compute an adjusted posterior is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for posterior inference regarding the efficacy of hypothetical treatment rules that modify ACT based on evolving cardiac function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for the estimation of the efficacy of treatment rules that modify ACT based on evolving cardiac function.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe the approach enabling inference/estimation of efficacy for dynamic ACT rules tied to cardiac function; the publication uses nearly identical framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum kernel methods can learn certain classically intractable functions efficiently if the kernel is well-aligned with the target function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum kernel methods can learn certain classically intractable functions efficiently if the kernel is well aligned with the target function.
alignments.rationale: The result about quantum kernel capacity given good alignment is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: General-purpose hyperparameter tuning techniques for quantum kernels lead to the kernel being well-approximated by a classical kernel.
alignments.pub_claim_text: General-purpose hyperparameter-tuning techniques for quantum kernels can lead to the kernel being well approximated by a classical kernel.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that general-purpose tuning can make quantum kernels approximable by classical kernels.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approximation of quantum kernels by classical kernels removes the possibility of achieving quantum advantage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approximation of quantum kernels by classical kernels removes the possibility of achieving quantum advantage.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion that classical approximation removes quantum advantage is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unless novel techniques are developed to control the inductive bias of quantum kernels, they are unlikely to provide a quantum advantage on classical data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unless novel techniques are developed to control the inductive bias of quantum kernels, they are unlikely to provide a quantum advantage on classical data that lacks special structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the negative claim by adding the condition "that lacks special structure," narrowing the scope of the original general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Moderators on Wikipedia Talk Pages engage in proactive moderation behaviors despite a lack of technical and social support. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Moderators on Wikipedia Talk Pages engage in proactive moderation behaviors despite a lack of technical and social support. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result verbatim, with no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automation could assist with the existing proactive moderation workflow of Wikipedia moderators. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automation could assist with the existing proactive moderation workflow of Wikipedia moderators. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about automation assistance is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feedback from moderators reveals both strengths and drawbacks of the prototype tool designed to assist in proactive moderation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feedback from moderators reveals both strengths and drawbacks of the prototype tool designed to assist in proactive moderation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Feedback findings are presented the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Concrete steps are suggested for further developing assisting technology to effectively support moderators. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Concrete steps towards further developing assisting technology for moderators are suggested based on the feedback received. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication offers the same suggested next steps based on feedback, with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multimodal learning for integrative multi-omics analysis can help researchers and practitioners gain deep insights into human diseases and improve clinical decisions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multimodal learning for integrative multi-omics analysis can help researchers and practitioners gain deep insights into human diseases and improve clinical decisions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion with no semantic change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several challenges are hindering the development of multimodal learning in multi-omics analysis, including the availability of easily accessible open-source tools. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several challenges are hindering the development of multimodal learning in multi-omics analysis, including the availability of easily accessible open-source tools. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The stated limitations are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This survey provides an up-to-date overview of the data challenges, fusion approaches, datasets, and software tools in the field of multi-omics analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This survey provides an up-to-date overview of the data challenges, fusion approaches, datasets, and software tools in the field of multi-omics analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey categorizes fusion approaches comprehensively to cover existing methods in the area of multi-omics analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey categorizes fusion approaches comprehensively to cover existing methods in the area of multi-omics analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The categorization claim is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The True Origin Model achieves promising accuracy at identifying origin locations in tweets, with 80% accuracy at the country level and 58% at the city level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The True Origin Model achieves promising accuracy at identifying origin location information in tweets, with 80% accuracy at the country level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the country-level 80% result but separates the city-level percentage into a different statement, so the original combined phrasing is clarified into distinct level-specific claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model utilizes machine-level natural language understanding to classify tweets based on their origin location information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not reiterate the described 'machine-level NLU' characterization of the model, so this specific framing appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A major concern with the current gold standard test set methodology is highlighted, indicating potential limitations in existing approaches. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A major concern with the currently regarded gold standard test set methodology is highlighted, indicating limitations in existing approaches. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding the gold-standard test set is restated with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a new data set and identifies further research avenues for advancing the area of spatial discourse analysis on Twitter. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research introduces a new data set and identifies further research avenues for advancing the area of spatial discourse analysis on Twitter. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim about the new dataset and future research directions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates varying levels of accuracy for different geographical levels, achieving 67% at the state level and 58% at the city level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an intermediate (state-level) accuracy result (67%) that was not present in the preprint; this is an added specific result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning potentials can be leveraged to identify low energy adsorbate-surface configurations more accurately and efficiently. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning potentials can be leveraged to identify low-energy adsorbate-surface configurations more accurately and efficiently. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about ML potentials is restated without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithm finds the lowest energy configuration 87.36% of the time while achieving a 2000x speedup in computation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm finds the lowest energy configuration 87.36% of the time while achieving a ~2000× speedup in computation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and speedup are identical; formatting differences do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We introduce the Open Catalyst Dense dataset containing nearly 1,000 diverse surfaces and 100,000 unique configurations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce the Open Catalyst Dense dataset containing nearly 1000 diverse surfaces and ~100,000 unique configurations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is the same; numeric punctuation/approximation changes do not alter the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method gives a robust recovery of the coefficients for differential equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates a robust recovery of the coefficients in differential equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the robust recovery claim with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates a significant denoising effect that can handle up to 100% noise-to-signal ratio for some equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieves a significant denoising effect, capable of handling up to 100% noise-to-signal ratio for some equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The denoising capability and 100% noise-to-signal claim are preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of narrow-fit and trimming mechanisms improves the recovery of coefficient support and values. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mechanisms of narrow-fit and trimming improve the coefficient support and value recoveries respectively. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies which mechanism affects support vs. value recovery, clarifying the original combined statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework is applicable to both ordinary and partial differential equations (ODEs and PDEs). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comprehensive set of numerical experiments validates the effectiveness of the proposed method for both ODEs and PDEs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames applicability as empirically validated through numerical experiments; this clarifies and supports the original applicability claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The weak formulation used in the proposed framework facilitates an efficient and robust way to handle noise. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an explicit claim about the weak formulation's role in noise handling that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our attacks can reconstruct full rows of a private dataset from aggregate query statistics and rank them by their odds of appearing in the private data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our attacks can reconstruct full rows of the private dataset D from aggregate query statistics Q(D).
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the reconstruction claim but separates the ranking into its own claim; the core reconstruction statement is maintained though presented more modularly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attacks significantly outperform those based only on access to a public distribution or population from which the private dataset was sampled.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our attacks significantly outperform those based only on access to a public distribution or population.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative performance claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The queries permit reconstruction of elements of the dataset, not the distribution from which the dataset was drawn.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The queries Q(D) allow for the reconstruction of elements of the dataset, rather than the distribution from which D was drawn.
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical; the publication preserves the distinction between dataset elements and the underlying distribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings illustrate the risks in releasing numerically precise aggregate statistics of a large dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings illustrate the risks in releasing numerically precise aggregate statistics of a large dataset.
alignments.rationale: The cautionary conclusion is carried over verbatim with no substantive change in scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Influence Maximization (IM) is a classical combinatorial optimization problem that aims at selecting a small number of users to maximize influence spread across online social networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Influence Maximization (IM) is a classical combinatorial optimization problem that aims at selecting a small number of users to maximize influence spread across online social networks.
alignments.rationale: The definitional statement is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional algorithms for solving the IM problem are categorized into heuristic and approximation algorithms, but they lack theoretical guarantees and are close to their performance limits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional algorithms for the IM problem can be categorized into heuristic and approximation algorithms, but they lack theoretical guarantees and are close to their performance limits.
alignments.rationale: The characterization of prior algorithms is preserved with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine Learning (ML)-based techniques have emerged as new methods to solve combinatorial optimization problems, offering fast solving speed and strong generalization ability to unknown graphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine Learning (ML)-based techniques have shown remarkable achievements in solving combinatorial optimization problems, offering fast solving speed and strong generalization ability to unknown graphs.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim (from 'have emerged' to 'have shown remarkable achievements'), increasing expressed confidence while keeping the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper reviews recent developments in ML-based methods, particularly Deep Reinforcement Learning, for solving the IM problem and its variants in social networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent developments in ML-based methods, particularly Deep Reinforcement Learning, provide a new direction for solving the IM problem and its variants in social networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the review statement to emphasize the methods as a new direction; content overlap is high though the phrasing is more forward-looking.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper identifies urgent challenges that need to be addressed in future research on Influence Maximization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are urgent challenges that need to be addressed in future research on Influence Maximization.
alignments.rationale: The identification of open challenges is preserved with similar wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MyoPS-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance in myocardial pathology segmentation across various scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MyoPS-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance in various scenarios for myocardial pathology segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the state-of-the-art performance claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture of MyoPS-Net effectively extracts and fuses cross-modal features from five-sequence cardiac magnetic resonance images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture of MyoPS-Net can flexibly combine five-sequence cardiac magnetic resonance images for effective myocardial pathology segmentation.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the model's cross-modal fusion ability; the publication frames it as flexible combination rather than feature-extraction wording, a minor clarification of mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MyoPS-Net demonstrates superiority and generalizability in handling complex combinations of different CMR sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MyoPS-Net demonstrates superiority and generalizability in handling complex combinations of different CMR sequences.
alignments.rationale: The superiority and generalizability claim is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of anatomical knowledge through a regularization module and inclusiveness loss improves segmentation results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of an inclusiveness loss in MyoPS-Net utilizes relations between myocardial scars and edema to improve segmentation results.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the inclusiveness loss and its relation-based mechanism while omitting explicit mention of the regularization module, narrowing the attributed cause of improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KGTN-ens is a framework that extends the Knowledge Graph Transfer Network to incorporate multiple knowledge graph embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework KGTN-ens incorporates multiple knowledge graph embeddings at a small cost.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the multiple-embedding extension and adds a note about low computational cost, clarifying implementation trade-offs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed KGTN-ens framework outperforms KGTN in terms of top-5 accuracy on the ImageNet-FS dataset for the majority of tested settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: KGTN-ens outperforms KGTN in terms of top-5 accuracy on the ImageNet-FS dataset for the majority of tested settings.
alignments.rationale: The performance advantage on ImageNet-FS is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new knowledge source, Wikidata embeddings, was constructed and evaluated with KGTN and KGTN-ens.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new knowledge source, Wikidata embeddings, is constructed and evaluated with KGTN and KGTN-ens.
alignments.rationale: The construction and evaluation of Wikidata embeddings is preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Daylight pedestrian crashes are associated with children (less than 15 years), senior pedestrians (greater than 64 years), older drivers (>64 years), and other driving behaviors such as failure to yield, inattentive/distracted, illness/fatigue/asleep.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Daylight pedestrian crashes are associated with children under 15 years, senior pedestrians over 64 years, older drivers over 64 years, and certain driving behaviors such as failure to yield and inattentiveness.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same associations though with slightly condensed phrasing; the substantive associations are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Young drivers (15-24 years) are involved in severe pedestrian crashes in daylight conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Young drivers aged 15 to 24 years are involved in severe pedestrian crashes in daylight conditions.
alignments.rationale: This demographic association is repeated verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pedestrian alcohol/drug involvement is the most frequent item in the dark-with-streetlight condition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pedestrian alcohol/drug involvement is the most frequent item in the dark-with-streetlight condition, particularly associated with specific pedestrian actions and driver age.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core finding and adds detail about associations with actions and driver age, a clarifying elaboration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fatal pedestrian crashes are associated with roadways with high-speed limits (>50 mph) during the dark without streetlight condition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fatal pedestrian crashes are associated with roadways that have high speed limits greater than 50 mph during dark conditions without streetlights.
alignments.rationale: The fatal-crash association with high-speed, unlit roadways is restated with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research findings are expected to provide an improved understanding of the underlying relationships between pedestrian crash risk factors and specific lighting conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research findings provide an improved understanding of the relationships between pedestrian crash risk factors and specific lighting conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication removes hedging ('expected to') and states the improved understanding more directly, increasing expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method classifies chemical compounds directly from NMR data without the need for structure elucidation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A proof-of-concept method for classifying chemical compounds directly from NMR data without performing structure elucidation has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result but frames it as a proof-of-concept development, which clarifies the claim rather than changing its meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of a convolutional neural network (CNN) is identified as suitable for the classification of chemical compounds from NMR data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method identified as suitable for classification is a convolutional neural network (CNN). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same assertion about CNN suitability with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Other methods, including clustering and image registration, have been found unsuitable for the task of classifying chemical compounds from NMR data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Other methods, including clustering and image registration, have not been found to be suitable for the task in a comparative analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions convey the same negative comparative finding about alternative methods; the publication adds that this arose from a comparative analysis but does not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate that deep learning can provide solutions to automation problems in cheminformatics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The result shows that deep learning can offer solutions to spectral interpretation problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the broad claim about automation in cheminformatics to the more specific area of spectral interpretation and lowers the stated certainty, constituting a revision with reduced confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a hierarchical complex systems framework to model AI risk. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a hierarchical complex systems framework to model AI risk and provide a template for alternative futures analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the original framework claim but adds that it can serve as a template for alternative futures analysis, which clarifies the contribution without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Survey data collected from domain experts indicate increased uncertainty over the powerful AI agent scenario. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Survey data collected from domain experts indicate increased uncertainty over the powerful AI agent scenario. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content of the claim is the same but the reported certainty is reduced in the publication, so the claim is unchanged semantically but the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is confidence in multiagent environments according to the survey results. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian neural networks (BNNs) can be used to formulate a virtual load monitoring framework that accounts for uncertainties in load predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian neural networks (BNNs) are proposed as a framework for virtual load monitoring, allowing for the detection of inaccurate load estimations for non-fully monitored wind turbines.
alignments.rationale: The published claim retains the core idea (BNNs for virtual load monitoring) but adds a specific capability (detection of inaccurate estimates), clarifying the application without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed virtual load monitoring framework has been thoroughly tested in an operational offshore wind farm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed virtual load monitoring framework using BNNs has been thoroughly tested in an operational offshore wind farm, demonstrating its effectiveness.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines testing and demonstration of effectiveness but conveys the same tested-result claim about the framework in an operational farm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the effectiveness of BNN models for fleet-leader-based farm-wide virtual monitoring.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed virtual load monitoring framework using BNNs has been thoroughly tested in an operational offshore wind farm, demonstrating its effectiveness.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's statement of effectiveness maps to the publication's sentence that the tested BNN-based framework demonstrates effectiveness; wording is merged in the paper's Q2.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BNNs intrinsically announce the uncertainties associated with generated load predictions, which is a significant advantage over deterministic models.
alignments.rationale: This explicit emphasis on the intrinsic uncertainty-reporting advantage is presented in the publication but does not have a direct standalone counterpart in the preprint (it elaborates on uncertainty benefits).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VIINTER is a method for view interpolation that uses the implicit neural representation of captured images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VIINTER is a method for view interpolation that uses the implicit neural representation of captured images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VIINTER enhances interpolation quality through several proposed techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VIINTER enhances interpolation quality through several proposed techniques.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VIINTER allows for view interpolation without the need for constructing 3D structure, estimating camera poses, or computing pixel correspondence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VIINTER allows for view interpolation without the need for constructing 3D structure, estimating camera poses, or computing pixel correspondence.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same conclusion about required inputs and assumptions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of VIINTER has been validated on several multi-view scenes with varying camera layouts and scene compositions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of VIINTER has been validated on several multi-view scenes with varying camera layouts and scene compositions.
alignments.rationale: Validation claim is restated without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VIINTER demonstrates the capability of implicit neural representations for view interpolation and suggests potential for image manipulation tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VIINTER demonstrates the capability of implicit neural representations for view interpolation and suggests potential for image manipulation tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this concluding remark unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAgNET is a novel encoder-decoder geometric deep learning framework that extends convolutional neural networks to accommodate arbitrary graph-structured data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAgNET is a novel encoder–decoder geometric deep learning framework that extends convolutional neural networks to accommodate arbitrary graph-structured data.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution described in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAgNET consists of innovative Multichannel Aggregation layers and graph pooling/unpooling layers, forming a graph U-Net architecture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAgNET consists of innovative Multichannel Aggregation (MAg) layers and graph pooling/unpooling layers, forming a graph U-Net architecture.
alignments.rationale: Same architectural components are described, with only minor typographic clarification of abbreviation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAgNET demonstrates predictive capabilities in surrogate modeling for non-linear finite element simulations in the mechanics of solids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAgNET demonstrates predictive capabilities in surrogate modeling for non-linear finite element simulations in the mechanics of solids.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Position-Aware Data-Efficient Learning framework for subgraph neural networks, called PADEL, addresses the challenges of data-efficient learning for subgraph prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Position-Aware Data-Efficient Learning framework (PADEL) addresses the challenges of data-efficient learning for subgraph prediction.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated with equivalent meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel node position encoding method introduced in PADEL is anchor-free and improves the learning of positional features in subgraphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel node position encoding method introduced in PADEL is anchor-free and improves the learning of positional features in subgraphs.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim; framing shifts classification to 'contribution' but content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new generative subgraph augmentation method based on a diffused variational subgraph autoencoder enhances the representation learning of subgraphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new generative subgraph augmentation method based on a diffused variational subgraph autoencoder enhances the representation learning of subgraphs.
alignments.rationale: Method and claimed effect are presented unchanged, though the publication labels them as contributions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiment results demonstrate the superiority of PADEL over state-of-the-art baselines in subgraph representation learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that PADEL outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on three real-world datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the outperforming claim but specifies it applies to three real-world datasets, narrowing and clarifying the original broader framing of 'extensive results.'

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simply scaling the width, depth, or dilation factor of existing architectures does not result in satisfactory performance when modelling audio effects such as fuzz and dynamic range compression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simply scaling the width, depth, or dilation factor of existing architectures does not result in satisfactory performance when modelling audio effects such as fuzz and dynamic range compression.
alignments.rationale: The reported negative result is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of time-varying feature-wise linear modulation into existing temporal convolutional backbones enables learnable adaptation of the intermediate activations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of time-varying feature-wise linear modulation into existing temporal convolutional backbones enables learnable adaptation of the intermediate activations.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach more accurately captures long-range dependencies for a range of fuzz and compressor implementations across both time and frequency domain metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach more accurately captures long-range dependencies for a range of fuzz and compressor implementations across both time and frequency domain metrics.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed visual quality control system for mineral wool achieved more than 98% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed visual quality control system for mineral wool achieved more than 98% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and asserted result are identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new model can recognize 20% more defective products compared to the current procedure used at the company. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new model can recognize 20% more defective products compared to the current procedure used at the company. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and quantitative improvement are the same in the publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Structural pruning methods were applied to the classifiers to create a light-weight and fast inference model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Structural pruning methods were applied to the classifiers to create a light-weight and fast inference model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is described identically in the publication, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The probability density of the birth-death governed by Kullback-Leibler divergence or by $\text{chi}^2$ divergence converges exponentially fast to the Gibbs equilibrium measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The probability density of the birth–death process governed by Kullback–Leibler divergence or by χ 2 divergence converges exponentially fast to the Gibbs equilibrium measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Statement is semantically equivalent (minor notation/formatting differences only) and asserts the same exponential convergence result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convergence rate to the Gibbs equilibrium measure is universal and independent of the potential barrier. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convergence rate to the Gibbs equilibrium measure is universal and independent of the potential barrier. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same universality claim about the convergence rate; wording and meaning are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Smooth and bounded positive solutions of the kernelized dynamics converge to the pure birth-death dynamics as the kernel bandwidth shrinks to zero. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Smooth and bounded positive solutions of the kernelised dynamics converge to the pure birth–death dynamics as the kernel bandwidth shrinks to zero. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same mathematical convergence statement with only British/American spelling differences; meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantitative estimates on the bias of minimizers of the energy corresponding to the kernelized dynamics are provided. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantitative estimates on the bias of minimisers of the energy corresponding to the kernelised dynamics are provided. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same quantitative-bias estimates; differences are only in spelling/formatting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The long-time asymptotic states of the kernelized dynamics converge towards the Gibbs measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Long-time asymptotic results on the convergence of the asymptotic states of the kernelised dynamics towards the Gibbs measure are proven. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert convergence to the Gibbs measure, but the publication explicitly frames these as proven long-time asymptotic results, which clarifies/formalizes the claim compared to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TorchFL is a performant library designed for bootstrapping federated learning experiments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TorchFL is a performant library designed for bootstrapping federated learning experiments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TorchFL provides ready-to-use abstractions for models, datasets, and federated learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TorchFL provides ready-to-use abstractions for models, datasets, and federated learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TorchFL enables researchers with little to no engineering background to set up federated learning experiments with minimal coding and infrastructure overhead. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TorchFL enables researchers with little to no engineering background to set up federated learning experiments with minimal coding and infrastructure overhead. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The usability/conclusion claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-moment fluid model constructed using either PINN or gPINN reproduces the time evolution of the electric field energy, including its damping rate, and the plasma dynamics from the kinetic simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multimoment fluid model constructed using either PINN or gPINN reproduces the time evolution of the electric field energy, including its damping rate, and the plasma dynamics from the kinetic simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim about reproduction of field energy and plasma dynamics; only minor formatting differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gPINN$p$-constructed multi-moment fluid model offers the most accurate results among the three approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gPINNp-constructed multimoment fluid model offers the most accurate results among the three approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the assertion that the gPINN-constructed model is the most accurate; content unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work sheds light on the accurate and efficient modeling of large-scale systems, which can be extended to complex multiscale laboratory, space, and astrophysical plasma physics problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work sheds light on the accurate and efficient modeling of large-scale systems, which can be extended to complex multiscale laboratory, space, and astrophysical plasma physics problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding scope and suggested extensions are preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model can predict lightning, hail, and heavy precipitation probabilistically on a 1 km resolution grid. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model can predict lightning, hail, and heavy precipitation probabilistically on a 1 km resolution grid with a temporal resolution of 5 min and lead times up to 60 min. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication combines spatial and temporal resolution into a single claim; the spatial prediction claim is preserved but clarified by adding temporal resolution and lead-time specifics (previously in a separate preprint claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model has a temporal resolution of 5 minutes and lead times up to 60 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model can predict lightning, hail, and heavy precipitation probabilistically on a 1 km resolution grid with a temporal resolution of 5 min and lead times up to 60 min. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The temporal-resolution and lead-time details are preserved but presented merged with the spatial prediction claim in the publication, so the claim is clarified/combined.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weather radar products are the most important predictors for lightning, hail, and heavy precipitation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weather radar products are the most important predictors for all three hazard types according to Shapley values analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about radar importance but clarifies the basis of the result (Shapley values analysis), making the claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CarDD is the first public large-scale dataset designed for vision-based car damage detection and segmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CarDD is the first public large-scale dataset designed for vision-based car damage detection and segmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact contribution claim verbatim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CarDD contains 4,000 high-resolution car damage images with over 9,000 well-annotated instances of six damage categories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CarDD contains 4,000 high-resolution car damage images with over 9,000 well-annotated instances of six damage categories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The dataset size and annotation details are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments conducted on CarDD demonstrate the effectiveness of state-of-the-art deep methods for car damage detection tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments conducted on CarDD with state-of-the-art deep methods provide comprehensive analyses highlighting the specialty of car damage detection. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the experimental claim to emphasize comprehensive analyses and the specialty of the task and lowers certainty to medium, so the core result is preserved but clarified and presented with somewhat tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Jointly training the ASR and EP tasks in a single end-to-end multitask model improves EP quality by leveraging information from the ASR audio encoder. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Jointly training the ASR and EP tasks in a single end-to-end multitask model improves EP quality by leveraging information from the ASR audio encoder. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is stated identically in the publication with unchanged emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method reduces median endpoint latency by 120 ms (30.8% reduction) and 90th percentile latency by 170 ms (23.0% reduction) compared to separate single-task models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method reduces median endpoint latency by 120 ms (30.8% reduction) compared to separate single-task models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the original combined latency claim and here the preprint claim is best matched to the median-latency statement; this narrows the focus although the numeric median reduction is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach does not regress word error rate while improving continuous recognition WER by 10.6% (relative). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For continuous recognition, word error rate improves by 10.6% (relative). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the same 10.6% relative WER improvement for continuous recognition; the meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-modal machine learning model, Air Quality Network (AQNet), successfully predicts three pollutants: NO$_2$, O$_3$, and PM$_{10}$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AQNet successfully predicts three pollutants: NO2, O3, and PM10. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This pollutant-prediction claim is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AQNet demonstrates improved prediction accuracy compared to a model that only uses satellite imagery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of supporting tabular data improves the predictions made by AQNet. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that supporting tabular data drive improvements (clarifying the source of gains) and reports medium certainty rather than the preprint's higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The addition of supporting data enhances the predictions made by the AQNet model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of supporting tabular data improves the predictions made by AQNet. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines "supporting data" to "supporting tabular data," clarifying the claim while preserving its medium confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When tested on out-of-sample data from the UK and Ireland, AQNet provides satisfactory estimates, although pollution metrics are overestimated by around 20%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed AQNet model provides satisfactory estimates on out-of-sample data from the UK and Ireland, though pollution metrics were overestimated by around 20%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The out-of-sample evaluation and ~20% overestimation are described equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DR-ALNS method outperforms vanilla ALNS, ALNS tuned with Bayesian optimization, and two state-of-the-art DRL approaches in solving an orienteering problem with stochastic weights and time windows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DR-ALNS method outperforms vanilla ALNS, ALNS tuned with Bayesian optimization, and two state-of-the-art DRL approaches in solving an orienteering problem with stochastic weights and time windows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DR-ALNS method achieves better performance with significantly fewer training observations compared to other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed DR-ALNS method achieves better performance with significantly fewer training observations compared to other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about sample efficiency is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DR-ALNS method is easily adapted to solve different routing problems and its learned policies perform consistently well across various instance sizes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DR-ALNS method is easily adapted to solve different routing problems and its learned policies perform consistently well across various instance sizes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The adaptability and consistent performance claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned policies from the DR-ALNS method can be directly applied to different problem variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned policies from the DR-ALNS method can be directly applied to different problem variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fine-grained Visual-Text Prompt-driven self-training paradigm (VTP-OVD) enhances the self-training paradigm with more powerful fine-grained alignment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fine-grained visual-text prompt-driven self-training paradigm (VTP-OVD) enhances the self-training paradigm with a more powerful fine-grained alignment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim aside from minor stylistic capitalization changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method enables the vision-language model (VLM) to obtain fine-grained alignment through learnable text prompts for an auxiliary dense pixel-wise prediction task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method enables the vision-language model (VLM) to obtain fine-grained alignment using learnable text prompts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shortens the wording (omitting explicit mention of the auxiliary dense pixel-wise task) but preserves the core result about learnable text prompts enabling fine-grained alignment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance for open-vocabulary object detection, achieving 31.5% mAP on unseen classes of COCO. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance for open-vocabulary object detection, achieving 31.5% mAP on unseen classes of COCO. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental performance claim and numeric result are identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed risk and scene graph learning method effectively forecasts trajectories of heterogeneous road agents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed risk and scene graph learning method effectively forecasts trajectories of heterogeneous road agents.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is textually the same as the preprint claim, keeping meaning, scope and confidence identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Heterogeneous Risk Graph (HRG) groups road agents and calculates their interaction adjacency matrix using a collision risk metric.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Heterogeneous Risk Graph (HRG) calculates interaction adjacency matrices based on an effective collision risk metric.
alignments.rationale: Both describe HRG producing adjacency matrices from a collision risk metric; the publication wording slightly clarifies phrasing (plural matrices, calls the metric 'effective') without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Hierarchical Scene Graph (HSG) models the driving scene by inferring relationships between road agents and the road semantic layout.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Hierarchical Scene Graph (HSG) models the driving scene by inferring relationships between road agents and the road semantic layout.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superior performance compared to other state-of-the-art approaches on multiple datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates comparable performance to other state-of-the-art approaches in trajectory forecasting.
alignments.rationale: The publication downgrades the performance claim from 'superior' to 'comparable', reducing its strength and narrowing the claim about empirical advantage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed learnable mask detector (LMD) effectively separates adversarial examples from genuine ones in automatic speaker verification (ASV).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed learnable mask detector (LMD) is an attacker-independent and interpretable method for separating adversarial examples from genuine ones.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same separation capability while adding interpretability and attacker-independence descriptors, clarifying the method's properties without weakening the claim of separation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LMD method is attacker-independent and requires only genuine examples for training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network used in the LMD requires only genuine examples for training, enhancing its attacker-independent nature.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the training-only requirement and links it explicitly to the neural network component, clarifying implementation details while preserving the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The score variation, defined as the absolute discrepancy between ASV scores of original and transformed audio, serves as an effective indicator for detecting adversarial examples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The score variation, defined as the absolute discrepancy between the ASV scores of an original audio recording and its transformed audio, is utilized as an indicator to detect adversarial examples.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes score variation as an indicator that is utilized for detection, removing the explicit adjective 'effective' and thus slightly tempering the strength while preserving the concept.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms five state-of-the-art baselines across 12 attackers and two ASV systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms five state-of-the-art baselines across 12 attackers and two representative ASV systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance-over-baselines claim but adds 'representative' to describe the ASV systems, clarifying the experimental setup without changing the result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extensive experimental results provide a benchmark for detection-based ASV defenses.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make or preserve the explicit claim that the experiments constitute a benchmark for detection-based ASV defenses; this claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) enhances the trust of end-users in machines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) enhances the trust of end-users in machines.
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing literature lacks a systematic and comprehensive survey on the use of XAI for IoT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing literature lacks a systematic and comprehensive survey on the use of XAI for IoT.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation claim from the preprint without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper presents the first holistic compilation on the development of XAI-based frameworks tailored for future IoT use cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents the first holistic compilation on the development of XAI-based frameworks tailored for future IoT use cases.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper illustrates widely-used XAI services for IoT applications, including security enhancement and Internet of Medical Things.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper illustrates widely-used XAI services for IoT applications, including security enhancement and Internet of Medical Things.
alignments.rationale: The described illustrative results are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses the cutting-edge development in edge XAI structures and their support for sixth-generation (6G) communication services for IoT applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper discusses the cutting-edge development in edge XAI structures and their support for sixth-generation (6G) communication services for IoT applications.
alignments.rationale: The discussion claim about edge XAI and 6G is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SF is able to maintain engagement better than other techniques in the literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Substitution of the fittest (SF) is a technique that effectively counters disengagement in two-population competitive coevolutionary genetic algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the engagement claim more narrowly to countering disengagement specifically within two-population competitive coevolutionary GAs, narrowing the original broader statement about other techniques in the literature.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resultant recommendations using SF are higher quality and more diverse than those produced by EvoRecSys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recommendations produced by SF are of higher quality and more diverse than those produced by EvoRecSys.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation-quality-and-diversity claim is effectively identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SF is domain-independent and requires no calibration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that SF performs similarly to alternative techniques but has the advantage of requiring no parameter tuning.
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the domain-independence assertion, reports similar performance rather than an outright advantage, and keeps the no-parameter-tuning point, thus narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SF maintains a better trade-off between engagement and performance than other techniques in the literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a novel claim about SF's trade-off between engagement and performance that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed distributed coordination strategies enable the estimation of a spatial phenomenon through collaborative adaptive sampling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed distributed coordination strategies enable the estimation of a spatial phenomenon through collaborative adaptive sampling.
alignments.rationale: Claim identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptive sampling algorithm is proven to be self-stabilising and locally optimal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptive sampling algorithm is proven to be self-stabilising and locally optimal.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical result is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulation results verify that the proposed algorithm effectively performs spatially adaptive sampling while maintaining a tunable trade-off between accuracy and efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulation results verify that the proposed algorithm effectively performs spatially adaptive sampling while maintaining a tuneable trade-off between accuracy and efficiency.
alignments.rationale: Simulation result claim is unchanged aside from a minor spelling variation ('tuneable'), which does not affect meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a unified end-to-end differentiable framework for multi-view, multi-frame hand tracking that directly predicts 3D hand pose in world space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework directly predicts 3D hand pose in world space.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution verbatim that the framework directly predicts 3D hand pose in world space with no substantive change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our system trained on a new large-scale egocentric hand pose dataset handles various challenging interactive motions.

(certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness of the LBM was explored with respect to practical variations in propagation distances and object structure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The robustness of the DLBM was explored with respect to practical variations in propagation distances and object structure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
