alignments.preprint_claim_text: MargNet is a deep learning-based classifier for identifying stars, quasars, and compact galaxies using photometric parameters and images from the SDSS DR16 catalogue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MargNet is a deep learning-based classifier for identifying stars, quasars, and compact galaxies using photometric parameters and images from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 16 catalogue.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution with expanded venue name but the substance of the claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MargNet is the first classifier focusing exclusively on compact galaxies and performs better than other methods to classify compact galaxies from stars and quasars, even at fainter magnitudes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MargNet performs better than other methods to classify compact galaxies from stars and quasars, even at fainter magnitudes.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the performance claim but omits the novelty/exclusivity language (“first” and “focusing exclusively”), so the core performance result remains but the claim about being the first/exclusive has been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model and feature engineering in such deep learning architectures will provide greater success in identifying objects in ongoing and upcoming surveys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model and feature engineering in deep learning architectures will provide greater success in identifying objects in ongoing and upcoming surveys.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about future applicability is carried over verbatim to the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MargNet minimizes the need for human intervention by learning classification directly from the data.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about reducing human intervention does not appear in the preprint and is newly stated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Temporal-aware bI-direction Multi-scale Network (TIM-Net) introduces a novel approach for modeling temporal patterns of speech emotions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Temporal-aware bI-direction Multi-scale Network (TIM-Net) introduces a novel approach for modeling temporal patterns of speech emotions.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution statement in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TIM-Net learns multi-scale contextual affective representations from various time scales.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TIM-Net learns multi-scale contextual affective representations from various time scales.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate that TIM-Net achieves 2.34% and 2.61% improvements in average UAR and WAR over the second-best method on six benchmark SER datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate that TIM-Net achieves 2.34% and 2.61% improvements in average UAR and WAR over the second-best method on six benchmark SER datasets.
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance improvements are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perona is a novel approach to robust infrastructure fingerprinting for big data analytics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perona is a novel approach to robust infrastructure fingerprinting for big data analytics.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perona employs common sets and configurations of benchmarking tools to enable direct comparability of benchmark metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perona employs common sets and configurations of benchmarking tools to enable direct comparability of benchmark metrics.
alignments.rationale: The methodology/result is restated without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perona discards insignificant benchmark metrics by learning a low-dimensional representation of the input metric vector.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perona discards insignificant benchmark metrics by learning a low-dimensional representation of the input metric vector.
alignments.rationale: The result about metric dimensionality reduction is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perona allows for context-awareness by taking previous benchmark executions into consideration, enabling the detection of resource degradation.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the preprint's claim about context-aware detection of degradation; instead it adds a different claim about using representations for configuration optimization.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Perona produces representations that can be used directly for resource configuration optimization.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim about direct use for configuration optimization is not present in the preprint and thus appears as an added result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NSGA-II is less effective when applied to problems with more than two objectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NSGA-II is less effective when applied to problems with more than two objectives.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about NSGA-II effectiveness is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NSGA-III computes the complete Pareto front of the 3-objective OneMinMax benchmark in an expected number of O(n log n) iterations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NSGA-III computes the complete Pareto front of the 3-objective OneMinMax benchmark in an expected number of O(n log n) iterations.
alignments.rationale: The technical result is stated the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NSGA-III shows a drastic advantage over the NSGA-II on the 3-objective OneMinMax benchmark.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NSGA-III shows a drastic advantage over the NSGA-II on the 3-objective OneMinMax benchmark.
alignments.rationale: The comparative conclusion is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mathematical arguments suggest that similar findings are likely for other benchmarks with three or more objectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mathematical arguments suggest that similar findings are likely for other benchmarks with three or more objectives.
alignments.rationale: The generalized mathematical implication is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI models can assimilate trends and patterns within the design parameter space to identify optimal material designs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI models can assimilate trends and patterns within the design parameter space to identify optimal material designs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are textually identical and convey the same result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper reviews approaches focusing on representation learning, structure-property-performance linkage estimation, and optimization/design exploration in materials-by-design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper reviews approaches focusing on representation learning, structure-property-performance linkage estimation, and optimization/design exploration in materials-by-design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The review scope and phrasing remain the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methods in the literature are evaluated based on their capacity to learn from limited data and their computational complexity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Methods in the literature are evaluated based on their capacity to learn from limited data and their computational complexity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Evaluation criteria are stated identically in both versions, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper suggests promising future research directions for energetic materials-by-design, including meta-learning and active learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper suggests future research directions such as meta-learning and active learning to enhance materials-by-design for energetic materials. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication wording removes "promising" and explicitly links the directions to enhancing materials-by-design, which clarifies intent without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PhysiQ continuously tracks and quantitatively measures people's off-site exercise activity through passive sensory detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not restate continuous off-site tracking via passive detection; no direct matching claim is present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework utilizes a novel multi-task spatio-temporal Siamese Neural Network to measure exercise quality. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports performance metrics but does not explicitly claim or describe the novel network architecture, so the specific architectural claim is not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PhysiQ evaluates exercises based on three different metrics: range of motions, stability, and repetition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PhysiQ digitizes and evaluates exercises in three different metrics: range of motions, stability, and repetition. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that PhysiQ also digitizes exercises and lowers certainty from high to medium, so the claim is clarified with tempered confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PhysiQ achieves an accuracy of 89.67% in detecting levels of exercise quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This quantitative performance result appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PhysiQ demonstrates an average R-squared correlation of 0.949 in similarity comparison. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported similarity performance metric is new in the publication and was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PhysiQ recognizes the nuances in exercises and works with different numbers of repetitions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This more detailed behavioral claim about handling repetition variability appears only in the publication and thus is added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPADE4 predicts the future trajectory of an observable variable without the knowledge of the other variables or the underlying system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPADE4 predicts the future trajectory of an observable variable without the knowledge of the other variables or the underlying system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication and unchanged in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPADE4 uses random features model with sparse regression to handle the data scarcity issue. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPADE4 uses random features model with sparse regression to handle the data scarcity issue. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Methodological description is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPADE4 outperforms compartmental models when applied to both simulated and real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPADE4 outperforms compartmental models when applied to both simulated and real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claim is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed kernel leads to a new GP surrogate that generalizes both the continuous relaxation and the Gower distance based GP models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new GP surrogate generalizes both the continuous relaxation and the Gower distance based GP models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same generalization claim about the GP surrogate; wording is effectively equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed GP model gives a higher likelihood and a smaller residual error than the other kernel-based state-of-the-art models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed GP model gives a higher likelihood and a smaller residual error than the other kernel-based state-of-the-art models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported superiority in likelihood and residual error is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is available in the open-source software SMT. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is available in the open-source software SMT. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Availability in SMT is stated identically in both documents.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed kernel extends continuous exponential kernels to handle mixed-categorical variables. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This specific characterization of the kernel extension appears as an explicit publication claim not separately stated in the preprint, so it is treated as added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Graph representation learning models can learn biased representations leading to discriminatory outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Graph representation learning models can learn biased representations leading to discriminatory outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concern about biased representations is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FairMILE is a novel framework that efficiently learns graph representations while enforcing fairness and preserving utility. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FairMILE is a novel framework that efficiently learns graph representations while enforcing fairness and preserving utility. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about FairMILE is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FairMILE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in terms of running time while achieving a superior trade-off between fairness and utility. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FairMILE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in terms of running time while achieving a superior trade-off between fairness and utility. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance and trade-off claims are consistent across both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Counterfactual Explanations can be leveraged to detect the important decision boundaries of a pre-trained black-box model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Counterfactual Explanations can be leveraged to detect the important decision boundaries of a pre-trained black-box model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in wording and meaning to the preprint claim, so no substantive change is observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The information from Counterfactual Explanations is used to build a supervised discretization of the features in the dataset with a tunable granularity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The information from Counterfactual Explanations is used to build a supervised discretization of the features in the dataset with a tunable granularity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An optimal Decision Tree can be trained using the discretized dataset that resembles the black-box model, but is interpretable and compact. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An optimal Decision Tree can be trained using the discretized dataset that resembles the black-box model but is more interpretable and compact. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the result with slightly different wording emphasizing 'more interpretable' rather than simply 'is interpretable' — a small clarification rather than substantive revision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results on real-world datasets show the effectiveness of the approach in terms of accuracy and sparsity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results on real-world datasets show the effectiveness of the approach in terms of accuracy and sparsity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result claim is carried over verbatim into the publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method estimates predictive uncertainty based on a single neural network without the need for an ensemble. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method estimates predictive uncertainty based on a single neural network, eliminating the need for an ensemble. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core contribution claim (single-network uncertainty estimation) with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The uncertainty estimates obtained from the proposed method match those obtained from deep ensembles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of uncertainty estimates from the proposed method matches those obtained from deep ensembles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms that the method's uncertainty estimates match deep ensembles; wording adds 'quality of' but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides uncertainty estimates with virtually no additional computational overhead over standard training and inference. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the specific strong claim about 'virtually no additional overhead' for standard training/inference; that specific assertion appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In an active learning setting, the proposed method's results match an ensemble-based strategy at an order-of-magnitude reduced computational cost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves results comparable to an ensemble-based strategy at a significantly reduced computational cost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports comparable results with reduced computational cost but uses milder phrasing ('significantly' vs 'order-of-magnitude'), indicating a tempered/clarified claim about the magnitude of cost savings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent unsupervised multi-object detection models may produce suboptimal object encodings for downstream tasks. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly include the preprint's critical statement about existing models producing suboptimal encodings; instead it focuses on the proposed scheme's improvements, so the limitation statement is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Motion and Object Continuity (MOC) scheme can be instantiated using any baseline object detection model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MOC training scheme can be applied to any object detection model baseline without developing an explicit deep architecture. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same applicability claim but softens certainty and frames it in slightly different terms (mentions no need for explicit architecture), so this is a clarification with reduced expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results show large improvements in the performances of a SOTA model in terms of object discovery, convergence speed and overall latent object representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating motion and object continuity enhances object discovery, convergence speed, and overall latent object representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same empirical findings about improvements in object discovery, convergence speed, and representations, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating motion and object continuity provides clear benefits for downstream tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate clear benefits for downstream reasoning tasks when incorporating motion and object continuity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the conclusion but specifies 'downstream reasoning tasks', which narrows the scope slightly while keeping the same level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature selection decreases the time complexity of the algorithm and reduces noise within the data, assisting in avoiding overfitting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature selection decreases the time complexity of the algorithm and reduces noise within the data, assisting in avoiding overfitting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent advancements in feature selection that leverage quantum annealing provide a scalable technique that maximizes predictive power while minimizing redundancy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advancements in feature selection that leverage quantum annealing provide a scalable technique that maximizes predictive power while minimizing redundancy. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved in wording but the publication lowers the stated certainty from high to medium, so the claim's confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm leveraging quantum annealing is expected to improve the bias/variance trade-off, yielding better features for training a statistical model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm leveraging quantum annealing is expected to improve the bias/variance trade-off, yielding better features for training a statistical model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same expectation/conclusion with the same expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate an advantage in utilizing features selected from the algorithm that leveraged quantum annealing compared to classical methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate an advantage in utilizing features selected from the algorithm that leveraged quantum annealing compared to classical methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is carried forward verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural network parametrizations can produce unreliable wave function approximations in variational Monte Carlo calculations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network parametrizations can produce unreliable wave function approximations in variational Monte Carlo calculations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random, persistent spikes in the energy estimate during training are a sign of failure in the neural network approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random, persistent spikes in the energy estimate during training are a sign of failure in the neural network approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over this diagnostic/result claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A collective-variable-based penalization yields a substantially more robust training procedure, preventing the formation of spurious modes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A collective-variable-based penalization yields a substantially more robust training procedure, preventing the formation of spurious modes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological/result claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The penalization scheme can be extended to other applications of variational Monte Carlo where a reasonable choice of collective variable is available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The penalization scheme can be extended to other applications of variational Monte Carlo where a reasonable choice of collective variable is available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same concluding claim about extensibility with the same expressed certainty.
